packages.overview: Picture yourself wandering through the emerald-carpeted rice terraces of Sapa at dawn, mist curling around your ankles like a whisper of adventure. With Travelxploria at your side, your journey through Vietnam becomes an unfolding story—one where every chapter brims with discovery. We’ll whisk you from the dragon-etched limestone karsts of Ha Long Bay aboard a traditional junk, to the lantern-lit alleyways of Hoi An, where you’ll learn to handcraft sweet rice cakes in a centuries-old family kitchen. In Ho Chi Minh City, our insider guides reveal hidden coffee cafés tucked behind peeling colonial façades, and by day three you’ll be gliding through the Mekong Delta’s emerald canals, trading smiles with fruit-orchard farmers. Finally, let us handle your sunset cocktail on Phu Quoc’s sugar-white sands as the sky blushes in farewell. From first-class flights and boutique stays to curated local encounters, Travelxploria orchestrates every detail—so you can simply savor Vietnam’s melody of flavors, stories, and timeless wonder.

Good morning, Da Nang! After enjoying breakfast at your hotel, Travelxploria’s representative and expert guide will pick you up for an unforgettable journey to Ba Na Hills, located just 25 km from Da Nang city center. Enjoy a scenic 35-minute drive to the Ba Na Hills Mountain Base, where your adventure begins. Upon arrival, check in at the Cable Car Station to experience the world’s longest cable car route. As you ascend, take in breathtaking panoramic views of the lush mountains, rolling hills, and the majestic Truong Son Mountain Range. Your first stop is the iconic Golden Bridge, an architectural marvel held up by giant stone hands, offering spectacular views of the surrounding landscapes. Continue exploring the charming Old French Villas, visit Nui Chua, and conquer the mountain peak at an altitude of 1,487 meters. Next, board the mountain climbing train to visit the Flower Garden, home to a vibrant collection of beautiful blossoms. Then, explore Linh Ung Pagoda, where Travelxploria’s guide will share insights into Buddhism and the cultural significance of this sacred site. Visit the Wine Tunnel, where you can see a collection of aged wines stored for many years. Enjoy a delicious buffet lunch at a restaurant before heading to Fantasy Park Amusement Center. Have fun with various thrilling games and 4D movies—most of the attractions are included in your tour package, except for games with prizes, which require an additional fee. After a fun-filled day, take the cable car back to the main gate and transfer to Da Nang city. On the way, pass by the famous Dragon Bridge for a quick photo stop before enjoying shopping at Han Market, a popular spot for souvenirs, handicrafts, and local specialties.

packages.overview:
• Arrival at Katunayake Airport, visit Munnewaram and Manawari Hindu Temples in Chillaw, then proceed to Sigiriya for an overnight stay after visiting Dambulla Golden Temple.
  • Climb Sigiriya Rock Fortress, visit a Spice Garden, explore the Temple of the Tooth, and enjoy a cultural show in Kandy.
  • Visit the Temple of the Tooth and Kandy View Point, then journey to Nuwara Eliya, stopping at Ramboda Waterfall and Hanuman Temple, with a boat ride option at Gregory Lake.
  • Travel to Ella, visit the Sita Amman Temple, and explore the Nine Arch Bridge and Ella’s scenic landscapes.
  • Visit the Murugan Temple in Kataragama, a significant Ramayana site, and explore its spiritual surroundings.
  • Stop at Ussangoda Ramayana Site, visit Rumassala Sanjeevani Mountain, explore Galle Dutch Fort, and relax at Hikkaduwa Beach.
  • Enjoy a boat ride on the Hikkaduwa River, visit Pancha Muga Anjaneyer Temple, and explore Colombo with shopping, a city tour, and visits to cultural landmarks.
  • Last-minute shopping or sightseeing in Colombo before departure, concluding the tour with heartfelt gratitude from Supreme Travels.

Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka, with a fascinating stop at a spice garden in Matale along the way. Learn about Sri Lanka’s world-famous spices, which have been treasured for centuries and remain a celebrated export today.

In the evening, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya, located just 5.5 km west of Kandy near the banks of the Mahaweli River. Renowned for its lush landscapes and impressive collection of orchids, the garden attracts over a million visitors each year.

At 4:45 PM, experience the spirit of Sri Lanka with a Cultural Dance Show, where traditional music, vibrant costumes, and age-old dance rituals come alive in an enthralling performance.

After the show, proceed to your hotel in Kandy for check-in and relaxation.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kandy.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

After breakfast, begin your scenic journey to Ella, one of Sri Lanka’s most enchanting highland destinations. Along the way, visit the sacred Seetha Amman Temple, located in the village of Seetha Eliya, just 1 km from the Hakgala Botanical Garden. According to the Ramayana epic, this is believed to be the place where Seetha was held captive by King Ravana, and today the temple stands as a place of devotion and historical significance.

Continue onwards to Ella, a serene hill country village nestled among lush greenery, misty mountains, and tea plantations. Known for its cool climate and laid-back charm, Ella is a haven for hikers and nature lovers alike.

Explore the highlights of Ella, including a visit to the iconic Nine Arch Bridge, a marvel of colonial-era railway construction set amidst dense jungle. Embark on a short hike to Little Adam’s Peak for panoramic views of the valley, and enjoy a relaxed Ella city tour, taking in the town’s cozy cafés, markets, and tranquil surroundings.

Dinner and overnight stay in Ella, surrounded by its peaceful, picture-perfect landscapes.

After breakfast in Ella, embark on a scenic drive to Kataragama, a journey of approximately 3 to 4 hours through Sri Lanka’s stunning southern landscapes. The route winds through tea plantations, lush forests, and rolling hills, offering breathtaking views along the way.

En route, make a stop at the enchanting Ravana Waterfall, one of Sri Lanka’s most beautiful and legendary cascades. Tied to the Ramayana epic, this waterfall is not only a natural wonder but also a site of mythological significance.

Upon arrival in Kataragama, visit the revered Murugan Temple, one of the island’s most important spiritual sites. Dedicated to Lord Murugan (also known as Kartikeya), the Hindu god of war and the son of Lord Shiva and Parvati, the temple draws devotees of multiple faiths, making it a powerful symbol of religious harmony.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kataragama.

Hikkaduwa is a charming coastal town on Sri Lanka’s southern shore, renowned for its golden beaches, vibrant coral reefs, and relaxed, beachside vibe. Its crystal-clear waters make it an ideal destination for snorkeling, diving, and soaking up the tropical sunshine.

A special highlight of Hikkaduwa is the chance to interact with baby turtles, which can often be seen along the public beach. These gentle creatures occasionally come close to shore, where locals and visitors alike can feed them small bits of food—a truly heartwarming and unforgettable experience for nature lovers and families.

Dinner and overnight stay in Hikkaduwa.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

On your final day, after breakfast, take the opportunity to enjoy some last-minute shopping or sightseeing in Colombo. As your unforgettable journey through the beauty and culture of Sri Lanka comes to an end, we at Travelxploria would like to extend our heartfelt gratitude for choosing us to be part of your adventure. It has been our pleasure to guide you through the wonders of our island, and we hope you leave with cherished memories.

Our Travelxploria Representative will assist you as you head to the airport for your departure. We wish you a safe and pleasant journey back home. Please do stay in touch, and we look forward to welcoming you again on your next visit to Sri Lanka.

End of the Tour.

Wishing you a safe journey!

packages.overview:
• Warm welcome at the airport followed by en route visits to a spice garden and Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage before reaching Kandy.
  • Full-day exploration of Kandy’s cultural treasures including the Temple of the Tooth, Royal Botanical Gardens, and a traditional cultural dance show.
  • Scenic drive to Nuwara Eliya with stops at Ramboda Waterfalls, Hanuman Temple, and visits to tea plantations and colonial landmarks.
  • Discover Ella’s natural wonders like Ravana Falls and Nine Arch Bridge before heading to Yala for an overnight stay.
  • Morning departure to Mirissa for leisure time on serene beaches and optional whale-watching adventures.
  • Explore the historic Galle Dutch Fort and lighthouse before enjoying the beaches and water sports in Bentota.
  • Return to Colombo for a city tour covering iconic sites like Galle Face, Pettah Bazaar, Colombo Museum, and the Lotus Tower.
  • After breakfast, transfer to the airport with cherished memories of your Sri Lankan adventure.

[{"city":"Kandy","country":"Sri Lanka","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Sri Lanka/Kandy/1744963452072-AdobeStock_82844404.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Sri Lanka/Kandy/1744963452962-AdobeStock_293028698.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Sri Lanka/Kandy/1744963453747-AdobeStock_364951258.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Airport to Kandy","day_summary":"

Upon your arrival at Colombo Bandaranaike International Airport, you will be warmly welcomed by our Travelxploria representative. After a brief introduction, you will be escorted to your comfortable vehicle to begin your exciting holiday in Sri Lanka.

Your first destination will be the beautiful city of Kandy.

En route, enjoy a complimentary visit to a Spice Garden, where you can discover the rich aromas and flavors of Sri Lankan spices.

You will also visit the renowned Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage, a sanctuary that cares for orphaned and injured wild elephants. Located in the village of Pinnawala, this facility hosts the largest herd of captive elephants in the world and has been a vital part of conservation efforts since its establishment in 1975 by the Sri Lanka Department of Wildlife Conservation.

After an enriching day of sightseeing, proceed to Kandy for check-in.

Overnight stay at your hotel in Kandy.

Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka, with a fascinating stop at a spice garden in Matale along the way. Learn about Sri Lanka’s world-famous spices, which have been treasured for centuries and remain a celebrated export today.

In the evening, visit the Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya, located just 5.5 km west of Kandy near the banks of the Mahaweli River. Renowned for its lush landscapes and impressive collection of orchids, the garden attracts over a million visitors each year.

At 4:45 PM, experience the spirit of Sri Lanka with a Cultural Dance Show, where traditional music, vibrant costumes, and age-old dance rituals come alive in an enthralling performance.

After the show, proceed to your hotel in Kandy for check-in and relaxation.

Dinner and overnight stay in Kandy.

Begin your scenic journey upcountry to Nuwara Eliya, often referred to as “Little England” for its cool climate and colonial charm. En route, visit the majestic Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea plantation and factory to witness the art of Ceylon tea-making, and the revered Sri Bhakta Hanuman Temple, nestled amidst the misty hills.

Arrive in Nuwara Eliya, perched at 6,200 feet above sea level, where you'll be enchanted by its old-world English charm—quaint cottages, beautifully manicured gardens, and the grand colonial architecture of the Grand Hotel. The city also features a historic golf course and hosts horse racing events, adding to its British countryside vibe.

Visit the Sita Amman Temple, a sacred site believed to be associated with the Ramayana epic, featuring vibrant statues and sculptures depicting the tales of Rama and Sita. Later, stroll around the scenic Gregory Lake, a picturesque reservoir set amidst the rolling tea hills, and unwind at Victoria Park, a beautifully maintained public park established during the colonial era.

After a fulfilling day, check in to your hotel and relax.Dinner and overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya.

After breakfast at the hotel, proceed to the charming town of Ella, located about 250 kilometers from Colombo in the scenic Badulla district. Famous for its breathtaking landscapes and connections to the legendary tale of the Ramayana, Ella offers stunning attractions such as the Ravana Falls viewpoint, the iconic Nine Arch Bridge, and the spectacular Ella Gap Viewpoint, which offers panoramic vistas of the surrounding hills. Enjoy leisurely excursions through this picturesque region before continuing your journey to Yala.

Overnight stay at a hotel in Yala.

After breakfast at the hotel, head to Mirissa, a picturesque town on Sri Lanka’s south coast in the Matara District. Located approximately 150 kilometers south of Colombo, Mirissa sits just four meters above sea level and is famed for its serene beaches and vibrant nightlife. Unlike the busier coastal resorts, Mirissa offers a more secluded feel, with two beautiful sandy bays divided by a rocky island accessible at low tide. The town is dotted with cozy bars and surf rentals, but its true highlight is the thrilling whale-watching tours available from May to November. During your visit, explore attractions such as the stunning Coconut Tree Hill, the hidden gem of Secret Beach, and embark on an unforgettable whale-watching adventure.

Overnight stay at a hotel in Mirissa.

​

After breakfast, set out to explore the scenic coastal city of Bentota, located on the southern shores of Sri Lanka. Known for its natural beauty and birdlife, Bentota is a haven for nature lovers, with opportunities to spot rare species such as the Indian Heron and various migratory birds along the riverbanks.

Begin your day with a Madu River boat ride, a serene journey through lush mangroves and vibrant wetlands. This ecological treasure is home to a wide variety of flora and fauna, including 70 species of fish, 50 types of butterflies, and over 100 species of birds—making it a paradise for wildlife and bird enthusiasts.

Next, visit the Kosgoda Turtle Hatchery, a vital conservation project dedicated to protecting Sri Lanka’s endangered sea turtles. Learn about the life cycle of turtles and witness the heartwarming sight of baby turtles being cared for before they are released into the sea.

In the evening, enjoy some thrilling water sports activities in Bentota, such as jet skiing, banana boat rides, or windsurfing, perfect for adventure seekers.

Dinner and overnight stay in Bentota.

​

After breakfast, set out to explore the scenic coastal city of Bentota, located on the southern shores of Sri Lanka. Known for its natural beauty and birdlife, Bentota is a haven for nature lovers, with opportunities to spot rare species such as the Indian Heron and various migratory birds along the riverbanks.

Begin your day with a Madu River boat ride, a serene journey through lush mangroves and vibrant wetlands. This ecological treasure is home to a wide variety of flora and fauna, including 70 species of fish, 50 types of butterflies, and over 100 species of birds—making it a paradise for wildlife and bird enthusiasts.

Next, visit the Kosgoda Turtle Hatchery, a vital conservation project dedicated to protecting Sri Lanka’s endangered sea turtles. Learn about the life cycle of turtles and witness the heartwarming sight of baby turtles being cared for before they are released into the sea.

In the evening, enjoy some thrilling water sports activities in Bentota, such as jet skiing, banana boat rides, or windsurfing, perfect for adventure seekers.

Dinner and overnight stay in Bentota.

Welcome to Colombo, the vibrant former capital and the largest metropolis in Sri Lanka. As the country's commercial and entertainment hub, Colombo is alive with modern skyscrapers, upscale restaurants, luxury spas, shopping malls, hotels, and a lively nightlife scene. Its wide, tree-lined boulevards add a unique tropical charm, making it both a bustling city and a serene escape.

Enjoy a leisurely stroll through Galle Face Green, a seaside promenade that offers some of the most breathtaking sunsets on the island. Sip on a cocktail at an open-air terrace as the sky transforms into a brilliant canvas of gold, orange, and purple over the Indian Ocean.

Treat yourself to a delicious lunch at the renowned Ministry of Crab (at your own expense), one of Asia’s top seafood restaurants, known for its fresh, flavorful Sri Lankan lagoon crabs.

After lunch, indulge in a shopping spree. Explore popular malls such as Majestic City, Liberty Plaza, and Crescat Boulevard, where you’ll find garments, books, handicrafts, and leather goods at competitive prices. For a more local experience, dive into the bustling Pettah Bazaar, where you can shop for a variety of items at bargain rates. Don't miss out on tourist favorites like ODEL and Arina, offering a wide range of fashionable apparel and souvenirs.


Check in to your hotel and unwind after a vibrant day in the city.Dinner and overnight stay in Colombo.

packages.overview:
Munnar

Hill station known for tea gardens and cool climate

  • Tea Plantations: Endless stretches of lush green tea estates.

  • Eravikulam National Park: Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr and Neelakurinji blooms.

  • Mattupetty Dam & Echo Point: Scenic picnic spots with boating and nature views.

  • Kundala Lake: Serene lake surrounded by rolling hills and pedal boating options.

  • Tea Museum: Insight into Munnar’s colonial tea heritage.

Alleppey

Famous for its tranquil backwaters and houseboat stays

  • Houseboat Cruise: Sail through the scenic backwaters, passing villages and paddy fields.

  • Alleppey Beach: Golden sands and a peaceful seaside vibe.

  • Backwater Canals: Ideal for a peaceful canoe ride or photo session.

  • Traditional Kerala Cuisine: Enjoy authentic meals served on a houseboat.

Varkala

Coastal town known for cliffside views and spiritual vibes

  • Varkala Cliff & Beach: Unique red cliffs overlooking the Arabian Sea, great for sunsets.

  • Janardanaswamy Temple: Ancient temple with spiritual significance.

  • Papanasam Beach: Believed to wash away sins – a peaceful spot for reflection.

  • Ayurvedic Spas: Rejuvenating massages and wellness treatments.

  • Cafes & Shopping: Laid-back cafes with sea views and local handicrafts along the cliff.

packages.overview:
• Kochi (Cochin) – Known as the Queen of the Arabian Sea, Kochi is a cultural melting pot where colonial charm meets coastal vibrance. Tourists are drawn to its historic sites like Fort Kochi, the Chinese Fishing Nets, and the Mattancherry Palace. The Jewish Synagogue and spice markets add to its multicultural appeal. Kochi also serves as the gateway to Kerala’s hill stations and backwaters.
  • Munnar – A dreamy hill station nestled in the Western Ghats, Munnar is famous for its endless tea plantations, cool climate, and misty hills. It's a haven for nature lovers and honeymooners, offering serene spots like Eravikulam National Park, Tea Museum, and Mattupetty Dam. Trekking, wildlife sightings, and scenic drives make Munnar a must-visit destination.
  • Alleppey (Alappuzha) – Often dubbed the Venice of the East, Alleppey is renowned for its tranquil backwaters and iconic houseboat experiences. A cruise through its lush waterways offers a glimpse into rural Kerala life. Tourists also enjoy Alleppey Beach, Ayurvedic wellness, and authentic Kerala cuisine served on board traditional kettuvallams.
  • Kanyakumari – Located at the southernmost tip of India, Kanyakumari is where the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and Indian Ocean meet. It's a spiritual and scenic destination known for the Vivekananda Rock Memorial, Thiruvalluvar Statue, and stunning sunrise/sunset views. Pilgrims, history buffs, and sea lovers alike are captivated by its unique coastal charm.
packages.structure_date:
[{"city":"Cochin","country":"India","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Arrival in Kochi & Exploring Colonial Heritage","day_summary":"

Upon arrival at Cochin International Airport, our Travelxploria representative will greet you and transfer you to your hotel. After check-in and freshening up, embark on a historical tour of Fort Kochi, visiting St. Francis Church, the iconic Chinese Fishing Nets, Jew Town, and the Paradesi Synagogue, along with the Mattancherry Palace (Dutch Palace) to admire its exquisite murals. In the evening, witness a mesmerizing Kathakali dance performance, an ancient classical storytelling art form, before indulging in a delicious seafood dinner at a heritage restaurant. Overnight stay in Kochi.

Start your day with a refreshing morning walk along Cherai Beach, a peaceful retreat with golden sands and gentle waves. The afternoon is free for leisure, where you can explore local markets, savor authentic Kerala cuisine, or opt for a visit to the Kerala Folklore Museum, which showcases Kerala’s rich cultural heritage. Alternatively, enjoy a sunset cruise along Kochi’s scenic coastline for a breathtaking view of the city’s waterfront. Overnight stay in Kochi.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Munnar","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Munnar/1744974631065-AdobeStock_128576315.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Munnar/1744974631812-AdobeStock_145526609.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Munnar/1744974631960-AdobeStock_382960364.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Munnar/1744974632051-AdobeStock_491596968.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Scenic Drive to Munnar ","day_summary":"

After breakfast, embark on a picturesque drive to Munnar, Kerala’s famous hill station, with rolling tea plantations and misty mountains. En route, stop at Cheeyappara & Valara Waterfalls, perfect for nature photography, and take a guided tour of a spice plantation to learn about Kerala’s famous cardamom, cinnamon, and pepper. Upon arrival, check into your tea estate resort and sip on a warm, freshly brewed cup of authentic Munnar tea. Spend the evening relaxing by a bonfire or exploring Munnar’s charming local market. Overnight stay in Munnar.

Begin your day with a guided walk through lush tea plantations, followed by a visit to the Tata Tea Museum to understand the fascinating process of tea-making. Later, explore Eravikulam National Park, home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr, and enjoy breathtaking views of rolling meadows and misty hills. Continue your journey to Mattupetty Dam, where you can go boating, and stop at Echo Point, where your voice reverberates across the hills. As the evening sets in, admire a stunning sunset over the scenic hills of Munnar. Overnight stay in Munnar.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Alleppey","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Alleppey/1744974652853-AdobeStock_45561458.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Alleppey/1744974653462-AdobeStock_472638122.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Alleppey/1744974653515-AdobeStock_604125231.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Transfer to Alleppey – Houseboat Cruise","day_summary":"

After breakfast, embark on a scenic 4-5 hour drive to Alleppey, Kerala’s backwater capital, famously known as the \"Venice of the East.\" Upon arrival, board a traditional Kerala houseboat for a luxurious cruise through the palm-fringed canals and vibrant villages. Enjoy a freshly prepared Kerala-style lunch on board featuring authentic local delicacies. Spend your afternoon sailing through lush landscapes, spotting local fishermen and traditional Kerala homes, and unwind with a cup of masala chai while watching the golden sunset over the backwaters. Overnight stay on the houseboat.

After breakfast, drive to Kanyakumari (6-hour journey), where the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and Indian Ocean meet, creating a mesmerizing confluence of waters. Upon arrival, check into your beachside hotel before heading out to explore Kanyakumari’s iconic landmarks, including the Vivekananda Rock Memorial, a serene meditation site, the towering Thiruvalluvar Statue, and the Kanyakumari Temple (Bhagavathy Amman Temple), one of the revered 108 Shakti Peethas. As the day ends, witness the breathtaking sunset at Kanyakumari Beach, a truly magical experience. Overnight stay in Kanyakumari.

packages.overview:
Dal Lake Houseboat Stay – Experience a serene retreat on still waters, surrounded by breathtaking Himalayan views and floating gardens.
Shikara Ride – Glide through the peaceful lake, soaking in the enchanting beauty and tranquil charm of Kashmir.
Nishat & Shalimar Bagh – Wander through mesmerizing Mughal gardens adorned with vibrant flowers and cascading fountains against majestic mountain backdrops.
Sonamarg Excursion – Marvel at golden meadows, snow-capped peaks, and alpine landscapes in this picturesque valley.
Gulmarg Gondola Ride – Ascend to panoramic heights, where thrilling adventures meet surreal snow-covered vistas.
Pahalgam Exploration – Discover lush pine forests, glistening rivers, and breathtaking countryside on this peaceful retreat.
Dachigam National Park Safari – Witness diverse wildlife, blooming orchards, and pristine landscapes in this untouched paradise.
packages.structure_date:
[{"city":"Srinagar","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Srinagar/1744974382647-AdobeStock_85975647.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Srinagar/1744974384506-AdobeStock_132109451.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Srinagar/1744974384675-AdobeStock_217809514.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Srinagar/1744974384796-AdobeStock_498916924.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Srinagar/1744974384972-AdobeStock_1267876471.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Arrival at Srinagar","day_summary":"

When you arrive at Srinagar Airport, you'll be welcomed and taken to your charming houseboat on Dal Lake. Spend the day at your own pace, soaking in the tranquil surroundings and calm ambiance of the lake. You can relax on the deck, admire the breathtaking views, or just enjoy the comfort of your distinctive lodging. This serene getaway provides a wonderful introduction to the beauty of Kashmir. Overnight stay on the houseboat.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Sheikh-ul-Alam International Airport","to":"Srinagar","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"3":[{"h_id":1241,"h_name":"Deluxe Houseboat","h_country":"India","h_city":"Srinagar","h_type":"Houseboat","h_rating":"3","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Srinagar/Deluxe Houseboat/1744886155829-main-image-of-houseboat.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Srinagar/Deluxe Houseboat/1744886167218-367659547.jpg\"}]"}],"4":[{"h_id":955,"h_name":"Executive Houseboat","h_country":"India","h_city":"Srinagar","h_type":"Houseboat","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Srinagar/Executive Houseboat/1744886263569-mughal-luxury-executive.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Srinagar/Executive Houseboat/1744886263690-queen-elizabeth-houseboat.jpg\"}]"}],"5":[{"h_id":1242,"h_name":"Premium Houseboat","h_country":"India","h_city":"Srinagar","h_type":"Houseboat","h_rating":"5","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Srinagar/Premium Houseboat/1744886329994-fdce23c03764c52a6b2f61c7302b65b3.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Srinagar/Premium Houseboat/1744886330034-hb-ambassador3.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Srinagar/Premium Houseboat/1744886330156-shiraz-houseboats-group.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Srinagar Local Sightseeing","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast, take an optional Shikara ride on Dal Lake, offering a peaceful start to your day. Visit Nishat Bagh, the \"Abode of Pleasure,\" followed by the enchanting Shalimar Bagh. These stunning gardens offer picturesque views with the Zabarwan Mountains in the background. On the way, explore a carpet-weaving factory before returning to your deluxe houseboat for an overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Sonamarg Day Tour","day_summary":"

After breakfast, enjoy an optional Shikara ride on Dal Lake before embarking on a full-day excursion to Sonmarg, the \"Meadow of Gold.\" Nestled in the Sindh Valley, Sonmarg offers stunning views of snow-capped mountains, alpine meadows, and lush forests. This peaceful retreat is perfect for photography and offers optional horse rides. After a delightful lunch, return to Srinagar and an overnight stay on your houseboat.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Gulmarg Day Tour","day_summary":"

After breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Gulmarg, the \"Meadow of Gold,\" discovered by the British in the 19th century. Known for its stunning ski slopes and the world’s highest golf course, it offers breath-taking views of Nanga Parbat (weather permitting). Drive from Tangmarg to Gulmarg, and you can enjoy a short trek to Khilanmarg or take a Gondola ride to Kongdori. After a day, return to Srinagar and relax with an overnight stay on the houseboat.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Pahalgam Sightseeing","day_summary":"

After breakfast, embark on an exciting excursion to Pahalgam, passing through Pampora's saffron fields and the historic Awantipura ruins. The journey leads you through dense pine forests and countryside to the serene confluence of Lidder River and Sheshnag Lake. Enjoy a local lunch, followed by bridle walks and photo opportunities with stunning mountain views.Take a horse ride in Pahalgam. Return to Srinagar for a relaxing overnight stay on the houseboat.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Visit to the Dachingham National Park","day_summary":"

After breakfast, head for Dachigam National Park. The Park is home to diverse wildlife and lush landscapes. Divided into lower and upper areas, it’s especially stunning in spring when apricots, peaches, and plums blossom. Enjoy a safari through this natural haven before returning to Srinagar. Check-in at your houseboat for a peaceful overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Departure from Srinagar","day_summary":"

After breakfast, you will be transferred to the Srinagar Airport for your onward journey. As you bid farewell to the enchanting landscapes of Kashmir, take with your unforgettable memories of the serene lakes, majestic mountains, and tranquil valleys.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Srinagar","to":"Sheikh-ul-Alam International Airport","splitStops":[]}}]}]}]

packages.overview:
Dal Lake Houseboat Stay – Experience luxury on Srinagar’s tranquil waters, surrounded by enchanting mountain reflections and floating gardens.
Sonamarg Excursion – Marvel at golden meadows, glacial streams, and snow-capped peaks in this breathtaking valley.
Thajiwas Glacier Adventure – Explore a pristine wonderland of snow and ice, with panoramic views of majestic mountains.
Manasbal Lake Retreat – Discover Kashmir’s deepest lake, a hidden gem reflecting the serene beauty of the surrounding landscapes.
Verinag Spring – Admire the octagonal marvel with crystal-clear waters, embraced by lush pine forests and vibrant flowers.
Gulmarg Gondola Ride – Ascend into the clouds for mesmerizing views of verdant meadows and towering Himalayan peaks.
Yousmarg Meadows – Wander through lush green pastures, surrounded by dense pine forests and awe-inspiring snow-capped peaks.
Mughal Gardens Tour – Stroll through Nishat and Shalimar Bagh, where cascading fountains and symmetrical terraces create timeless beauty.
Pahalgam Valley – Embrace nature’s charm at the confluence of Lidder River, amid rolling meadows and pine-covered slopes.
Baisaren Village – A peaceful retreat nestled in towering fir and pine forests, offering untouched tranquility and rustic charm.

Srinagar","day_summary":"

After breakfast, head to Manasbal Lake. Known as the deepest lake in Kashmir, it is often referred to as a \"gem\" among the region's lakes. The serene waters and picturesque surroundings offer a peaceful retreat, especially during summer when the lake forms secure ice layers. Explore the beauty of this tranquil spot before returning to Srinagar in the evening for a comfortable overnight stay at your hotel.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Day trip to Verinag & Stay at Srinagar","day_summary":"

After breakfast, proceed to Verinag, home to the serene Verinag Spring. The spring's octagonal shape is surrounded by tall pine trees and vibrant flowers. The sparkling clear waters reflect the calm beauty of the area, creating a peaceful atmosphere. After exploring this natural wonder, return to Srinagar in the evening for an overnight stay at your hotel.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Day trip to Gulmarg & Stay at Srinagar","day_summary":"

Early in the morning, drive through willow trees, fast-flowing streams, and lush meadows towards Gulmarg, the \"Meadow of Flowers.\" Surrounded by towering snow-capped peaks and dense coniferous forests, it offers stunning views at every turn. Stroll or take a pony ride to the world’s highest golf course, and experience the thrill of the Gondola lift. After a day filled with natural beauty and adventure, return to Srinagar in the evening for a overnight stay at your hotel.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Day trip to Yousmarg & Stay at Srinagar","day_summary":"

After breakfast, drive to Yousmarg, located 50 km from Srinagar in the Badgam district. The two-hour drive is a treat, passing through lush green meadows, dense pine forests, and breathtaking snow-capped mountains. Yousmarg, with its peaceful ambiance, is perfect for picnics and long walks amidst nature’s beauty. In the evening, return to Srinagar for an overnight stay at your hotel.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Srinagar Local Sightseeing & Stay at Srinagar","day_summary":"

After breakfast, set off to explore the Mughal gardens of Srinagar Nishat Bagh and Shalimar Bagh, both nestled on the banks of Dal Lake. Nishat Bagh, the larger garden, boasts a beautiful terrace lined with Chinar trees. These gardens are masterpieces of symmetrical design, with central waterways and fountains dividing the lush terraces. Return to your hotel in Srinagar for the night.

Upon arrival in Pahalgam, take a leisurely walk to Baisaren, a charming village nestled in pine and fir forests. Enjoy a peaceful overnight stay at hotel in Pahalgam.

Return to your hotel for an overnight stay, with the evening free for leisure.

packages.overview:
​Dal Lake – Glide on a peaceful shikara ride, surrounded by snow-capped peaks and traditional houseboats in Srinagar’s serene waters.
Awantipura Ruins – Discover the ancient stone carvings of this 9th-century temple, whispering tales of Kashmir’s rich heritage.
Betaab Valley – Wander through lush meadows and crystal-clear streams, where Bollywood’s romance meets breathtaking landscapes.
Gulmarg Gondola – Soar above snow-laden slopes on one of the world’s highest cable cars, unveiling majestic mountain views.
Mughal Gardens – Stroll through terraced gardens, where cascading fountains and vibrant flowers frame Dal Lake’s stunning beauty.
Tulip Garden – If in season, witness a riot of colors, as thousands of tulips bloom against the backdrop of Kashmir’s hills.
Thajiwas Glacier – Embark on an adventurous pony ride to this snowy paradise, embraced by towering Himalayan peaks.
Lal Chowk Market – Immerse yourself in Kashmir’s bustling culture, shopping for handwoven shawls, saffron, and exquisite handicrafts.
Shankaracharya Temple – Climb to this sacred hilltop shrine, where panoramic city views and a spiritual aura await.

Glide across the serene waters, surrounded by floating gardens, majestic snow-capped mountains, and beautifully crafted houseboats. The gentle rhythm of the water, the cool breeze, and the golden hues of the evening sky create a magical atmosphere. As twilight descends, return to your houseboat for a cozy stay, embracing the peaceful charm of Srinagar’s enchanting landscapes.

Grand/1742457061439-Welcomheritage 2.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Srinagar/WelcomHeritage Grand/1742457061561-Welcomheritage 4.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Srinagar Local Sightseeing","day_summary":"

Explore the beauty of Srinagar with visits to its famous attractions. Start with the stunning Tulip Garden (if in season), where vibrant blooms create a mesmerizing landscape. Next, visit the Mughal Mosque, showcasing exquisite architecture and peaceful surroundings. Take in the cultural richness of the city, with its mix of gardens, history, and local charm. In the evening, check into Srinagar hotel for an overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Visit to Sonmarg","day_summary":"

After breakfast, depart for Sonmarg, known as \"The Meadow of Gold,\" located 84 km from Srinagar at an altitude of 9,000 feet. Surrounded by majestic mountains and vibrant flowers, Sonmarg is a paradise for nature lovers. You can hire ponies to visit the Thajiwas Glacier, a popular attraction during the summer months. After a day of scenic exploration, return to Srinagar for a relaxing evening and an overnight stay at Srinagar hotel.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Depature Day ","day_summary":"

On the final day of your journey, wake up to the tranquil beauty of Dal Lake and enjoy a leisurely breakfast, savoring the last flavors of Kashmir. Our Travelxploria representative will be there to assist you with a seamless departure. If time allows, take a quiet stroll by the lake or explore a local market for some last-minute souvenirs perhaps a handwoven Kashmiri shawl or aromatic saffron. As you bid farewell to the snow-capped peaks, lush valleys, and peaceful houseboats, cherish the unforgettable memories. With a heart full of warmth and nostalgia, depart Srinagar.

packages.overview:
• ​Arrival at Bagdogra Airport, transfer to Phuentsholing (5 hrs), check-in, and free time for the local market.
  • Drive from Phuentsholing to Thimphu (6 hrs), immigration formalities, and evening free for the local market.
  • Explore Thimphu, visiting the National Memorial Chorten, Buddha Dordenma Statue, Changangkha Lhakhang, Motithang Takin Preserve, National Library, Painting School, Bhutan Textile Museum, Clock Tower, and Tashi Chho Dzong.
  • ​Drive from Thimphu to Paro via Dochula Pass and Punakha, visiting Punakha Dzong and Punakha Suspension Bridge.
  • Local sightseeing in Paro, visiting Simtokha Dzong, National Museum, Ta Dzong, Rinpung Dzong, Drukgyal Dzong, Paro Airport View, and Kyichu Lhakhang.
  • Drive from Paro to Phuentsholing (6 hrs), evening free for the local market.
  • Departure from Phuentsholing, transfer to NJP Station/Bagdogra Airport, cherishing Bhutan Holiday Tour memories.​

Overnight stay at the hotel in Thimphu.

In Thimphu, you can explore several significant landmarks, starting with the National Memorial Chorten, built in memory of the third Druk Gyalpo and dedicated to World Peace, a popular site for Buddhist religious festivals. The Buddha Dordenma Statue, located atop a hill, is a massive golden Buddha with 125,000 smaller Buddha statues inside, making it an iconic spot in the city. Changangkha Lhakhang, a 12th-century temple known as the \"wish-fulfilling\" temple, offers a stunning view of Thimphu. The Motithang Takin Preserve, home to Bhutan's national animal, the Takin, is a great destination for animal lovers. The National Library, established in 1967, houses a valuable collection of historic manuscripts, photos, and books. The Painting School, or Institute for Zorig Chusum, is where students learn traditional art forms, while the Bhutan Textile Museum showcases the country’s rich textile heritage. The Clock Tower, a unique architectural marvel with four clock faces, is a popular site, especially in the evening when visitors can enjoy the nearby restaurants and souvenir shops. Finally, Tashi Chho Dzong, a monastery on the banks of the Wang Chhu River, hosts the annual three-day Tsechu festival and dates back to 1216 AD. Overnight stay at the hotel in Thimphu.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Paro","country":"Bhutan","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618052142-AdobeStock_65598899.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618052760-AdobeStock_137774984.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618052855-AdobeStock_294984667.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618053002-AdobeStock_526226645.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618053279-AdobeStock_699351803.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618053406-AdobeStock_1070936357.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Thimpu – Punakha sightseeing to Paro","day_summary":"

Dochula Pass, located between Thimphu and Punakha at an elevation of around 3100 meters, offers a stunning view of the Eastern Himalayas and is home to 108 stupas. In winter, the pass is covered in snow, making it a particularly beautiful destination. Punakha Dzong, regarded as one of Bhutan's most beautiful architectural wonders, is situated at the confluence of the Pho Chu and Mo Chu rivers. It is a sacred site with deep historical significance and is especially suitable for first-timers, children, and elderly visitors. The Punakha Suspension Bridge, one of the oldest of its kind in the world, was built by the Buddhist monk Thangtong Gyalpo and is adorned with prayer flags. The bridge offers a picturesque spot for bird watching and nature photography. Overnight stay at the hotel in Paro.

Simtokha Dzong: Built in 1629 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal, Simtokha Dzong is one of the oldest dzongs in Bhutan. Also known asSangak Zabdhon Phodrang, it holds great historical and religious significance. Constructed in the 17th century, this ancient fortress is a remarkable example of Bhutanese architecture and remains an important cultural landmark.

National Museum of Bhutan: Once an ancient watchtower, this museum now houses an extensive collection of Bhutanese artifacts. From traditional costumes and armor to weapons and daily life tools, the museum reflects the rich heritage and artistic traditions of Bhutan.

Ta Dzong: Originally built in 1649 as a watchtower to defend the Rinpung Dzong during times of war, Ta Dzong was later converted into Bhutan’s National Museum in 1968. It now serves as a cultural treasure trove, offering insights into the country’s past.

Rinpung Dzong: Also known as Paro Dzong, this majestic fortress was constructed in 1644 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal. It is an excellent example of traditional Bhutanese architecture and is one of Paro’s top tourist attractions.

Drukgyal Dzong: Known as the “Victorious Fortress,” Drukgyal Dzong is a historic Buddhist monastery and fortress. It commemorates Bhutan’s victory over Tibetan invaders and is considered one of the most beautiful archaeological sites in the country.

Paro Airport Viewpoint: Paro International Airport, Bhutan’s only international airport, is famous for its scenic beauty. Surrounded by dramatic mountains and lush valleys, it is considered one of the most stunning airports in the world and a must-visit spot for travelers in Paro.

Kyichu Lhakhang: Also called Kyerchu Lhakhang, this sacred temple is one of the oldest and most revered pilgrimage sites in Bhutan. It is part of a network of 108 temples built by a Tibetan king and is a cherished spiritual destination known for its serene beauty.

The National Memorial Chorten, built in memory of the third Druk Gyalpo, is dedicated to World Peace and is popular among locals for various Buddhist religious festivals. Another notable site in Thimphu is the Buddha Dordenma Statue, a massive golden Buddha atop a hill with 125,000 smaller Buddhas hidden inside, offering panoramic views and often referred to as Buddha Point. Changangkha Lhakhang, a 12th-century temple, is known as the wish-fulfilling temple and offers stunning views of Thimphu city. For animal lovers, the Motithang Takin Preserve is a great spot to see Bhutan's national animal, the takin, in a preserved setting. The National Library, established in 1967, houses historic manuscripts, photos, and books, while the Institute for Zorig Chusum, or Painting School, teaches traditional arts and crafts. The Bhutan Textile Museum showcases the rich history of Bhutanese textiles and clothing. The Clock Tower Square, with its four-faced clock tower, is an architectural marvel and a popular gathering spot with many nearby restaurants and souvenir shops. Tashi Chho Dzong, a monastery located next to the Wang Chhu River, is famous for its annual Tsechu festival and was built in 1216 A.D. Overnight stay at a hotel in Thimphu.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Paro","country":"Bhutan","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618052142-AdobeStock_65598899.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618052760-AdobeStock_137774984.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618052855-AdobeStock_294984667.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618053002-AdobeStock_526226645.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618053279-AdobeStock_699351803.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Bhutan/Paro/1744618053406-AdobeStock_1070936357.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Thimpu – Punakha sightseeing to Paro","day_summary":"

Dochula Pass, located between Thimphu and Punakha at an elevation of 3100 meters, offers stunning views of the Eastern Himalayas. The pass is home to 108 stupas, and in winter, it is covered in snow, creating a beautiful scene. The Punakha Dzong, situated at the confluence of the Pho Chu (male) and Mo Chu (female) rivers, is considered one of the most beautiful dzongs in Bhutan. It is particularly accessible for first-timers, children, and elderly visitors, and offers a chance to explore the sacred history of this architectural marvel. Another highlight in Punakha is the Punakha Suspension Bridge, one of the oldest suspension bridges in the world, believed to have been built by the Buddhist monk Thangtong Gyalpo. This historic bridge, adorned with prayer flags, provides a perfect spot for birdwatching and nature photography, making it renowned for its picturesque appearance. Overnight stay at a hotel in Paro.

Simtokha Dzong: Built in 1629 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal, Simtokha Dzong is one of the oldest dzongs in Bhutan. Also known asSangak Zabdhon Phodrang, it holds great historical and religious significance. Constructed in the 17th century, this ancient fortress is a remarkable example of Bhutanese architecture and remains an important cultural landmark.

National Museum of Bhutan: Once an ancient watchtower, this museum now houses an extensive collection of Bhutanese artifacts. From traditional costumes and armor to weapons and daily life tools, the museum reflects the rich heritage and artistic traditions of Bhutan.

Ta Dzong: Originally built in 1649 as a watchtower to defend the Rinpung Dzong during times of war, Ta Dzong was later converted into Bhutan’s National Museum in 1968. It now serves as a cultural treasure trove, offering insights into the country’s past.

Rinpung Dzong: Also known as Paro Dzong, this majestic fortress was constructed in 1644 by Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal. It is an excellent example of traditional Bhutanese architecture and is one of Paro’s top tourist attractions.

Drukgyal Dzong: Known as the “Victorious Fortress,” Drukgyal Dzong is a historic Buddhist monastery and fortress. It commemorates Bhutan’s victory over Tibetan invaders and is considered one of the most beautiful archaeological sites in the country.

Paro Airport Viewpoint: Paro International Airport, Bhutan’s only international airport, is famous for its scenic beauty. Surrounded by dramatic mountains and lush valleys, it is considered one of the most stunning airports in the world and a must-visit spot for travelers in Paro.

Kyichu Lhakhang: Also called Kyerchu Lhakhang, this sacred temple is one of the oldest and most revered pilgrimage sites in Bhutan. It is part of a network of 108 temples built by a Tibetan king and is a cherished spiritual destination known for its serene beauty.

packages.overview:
Assam: A northeastern state of India known for its tea gardens, wildlife, natural beauty, and cultural diversity.

Shillong: Known as the “Scotland of the East,” with rolling hills, waterfalls, and attractions like Ward’s Lake, Elephant Falls, and the Shillong Peak.

Cherrapunjee: Famous for its heavy rainfall, stunning living root bridges, Nohkalikai Falls, and picturesque landscapes.

Kaziranga: A UNESCO World Heritage site, home to the Indian one-horned rhinoceros, and offers safari experiences in its vast grasslands.

Guwahati: Assam's largest city, known for Kamakhya Temple, Brahmaputra River, and vibrant markets.

packages.overview:
Assam: A northeastern gem known for its tea gardens, wildlife, and rich cultural diversity.

Shillong: The “Scotland of the East,” known for its rolling hills, picturesque waterfalls, Shillong Peak, and serene lakes like Ward’s Lake.

Kaziranga: A UNESCO World Heritage site, home to the endangered Indian one-horned rhinoceros, offering safaris through its vast grasslands.

Guwahati: Assam’s largest city, famous for the Kamakhya Temple, Umananda Island, and the scenic Brahmaputra River.

packages.package_name: The Best of Seven Sister Tour
packages.overview:
Assam & Meghalaya: A mesmerizing mix of wildlife, spirituality, natural beauty, and cultural heritage in Northeast India.

Guwahati: The largest city in Assam, home to the sacred Kamakhya Temple, scenic Brahmaputra River cruises, and Assam State Museum.

Shillong: The "Scotland of the East," offering attractions like Shillong Peak, Elephant Falls, Ward’s Lake, and a vibrant café culture.

Cherrapunji: One of the wettest places on Earth, known for living root bridges, Nohkalikai Falls, Mawsmai Caves, and misty landscapes.

Kaziranga: A UNESCO World Heritage site, famous for the one-horned rhinoceros, thrilling jungle safaris, and diverse wildlife.

The air hums with devotion as the ancient shrine, dedicated to Goddess Kamakhya, welcomes you with its mystical aura.

Lakes to Lively Lanes","day_summary":"


Step into the embrace of Shillong’s cool breeze and rolling hills. Start with a peaceful visit to Umiam Lake, where waters reflect the emerald pines. Glide on a boat or simply bask in the tranquil surroundings. Later, let the lively spirit of Police Bazar enchant you. The bustling streets, brimming with shops, cafes, and local treasures, invite you to Shillong’s culture.

The road to Cherrapunji unfolds like a dream, with hills and cottony clouds guiding your path. Soon, the majestic Seven Sisters Falls greets you with its dramatic, seven-tiered cascade—a symphony of nature's power and grace. Watch the mist rise from the plunging waters and let the cool breeze carry your worries away. 

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Cherrapunji’s Hidden Marvels","day_summary":"

Embark on an unforgettable trek through dense forests to witness the iconic Double Decker Living Root Bridge—a masterpiece woven by nature and time. Feel the magic of the ancient roots beneath your feet. After your adventure, wander through Cherrapunji’s vibrant local markets, brimming with handmade crafts, fresh produce, and local treats. Engage with friendly locals and savor the flavors of Meghalaya, capturing the true spirit of this rain-kissed paradise.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Kaziranga","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Kaziranga/1744988690731-AdobeStock_681704462.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Kaziranga/1744988692601-AdobeStock_780229704.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Kaziranga/1744988692740-AdobeStock_982234602.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Kaziranga/1744988692933-AdobeStock_1379724291.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"A Kaziranga Expedition","day_summary":"

Journey to the Kaziranga National Park, where nature reigns supreme. As the afternoon sun casts a golden hue, hop into a jeep for an exhilarating safari. Traverse through tall grasslands and dense forests, keeping your eyes peeled for the majestic one-horned rhinoceros, playful elephants, and vibrant birdlife. The sounds of the wild—the rustling leaves and distant calls—immerse you in nature’s untamed symphony.

Serenity","day_summary":"

Start your day with a peaceful elephant safari, where the morning mist adds a mystical charm to Kaziranga’s wilderness. Towering over the grasslands, spot the iconic one-horned rhinoceros, wild buffalo, and vibrant birdlife up close. Later, indulge in a leisurely afternoon—sip a warm cup of Assam tea, read by the riverside, or simply soak in the calm, wild beauty that surrounds you.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Farewell to the Journey","day_summary":"

As your journey through Meghalaya and Assam comes to a close, travel back to Guwahati with a heart full of memories. With a final glimpse of the mighty Brahmaputra, bid farewell to the land of clouds and legends. Though your adventure ends here, the spirit of the Northeast lingers—whispering promises of new horizons and future returns.

Vietnam Tour from 11th Feb '25 to 20th Feb'25.

Vietnam tour was dream for us for long times. At last we could fulfill our dream with precise guidance of our tour operator Travelxploria.

We started our tour from Kolkata to Ho Chi Minh City (southern Vietnam) to Da Nang (Central Vietnam) and ended up at Hanoi (Northern Vietnam) to Kolkata, covering Cu Chi tunnels tour, Mackong Delta tour, Hop On Hop Off Ho Chi Minh City Bus tour, Marble Mountain tour, Hoi An Lantern Boat ride, Ba Na hill Golden Bridge, French Village tour, Da Nang Dragon Bridge Fire Show, Halong Bay Cruise night stay, Sampan Boat ride, Trung Trang Cave tour, Ninh Binh Ancient Capital tour, Tam Coc Boat ride, Hanoi Cycle Rickshaw city tour etc.

We have been accomodated in premium Hotels and Cruise and moreover liasoning of the tour itinerary in skill hands by our tour operator Travelxploria made the tour Hassle Free, Memorable, Fascinating and Mind Blowing.

Special thanks to CEO of Travelxploria, Ms.Riya Madam, tour cordinator Ms. Jenny from Vietnam and Mr. Bikram Mishra for their experienced organising skill made our tour remarkable.
We wish to travel with Travelxploria in ensuing days for exploring other destinations.

overview:
Under 50,000 Wonders: Big Journeys on Small Budgets with Travelxploria

Who says extraordinary experiences need an extravagant budget? At Travelxploria, we believe wanderlust shouldn’t wait—and with our Under 50,000 collection, you can discover breathtaking destinations, rich cultures, and unforgettable adventures without stretching your wallet. Whether it’s the golden sands of Dubai, the tropical charm of Southeast Asia, or the vibrant corners of India, these travel dreams are now within easy reach.

With the expert planning of the Best Travel Agency and the trusted care of your Best Tour Operator, each handpicked itinerary under ₹50,000 blends value with comfort, exploration with ease. From the bustling street markets of Bangkok to tranquil houseboat stays in Kerala, from the shimmering skylines of Dubai to the serene rice fields of Bali, we turn your tight budget into a wide canvas of possibilities.

Travelxploria’s thoughtfully curated budget tour packages include iconic landmarks, immersive cultural experiences, local flavors, and comfortable stays—crafted with the same care and service we bring to every luxury escape. Whether you're a solo traveler, a couple seeking affordable romance, or a family looking for value-packed holidays, our Best Family Package and Best Honeymoon Planner options in this range ensure something for everyone.

Don't just travel—travel smart. With Travelxploria, your dream vacation doesn’t wait for the perfect time; it begins now, under 50,000.

Let every rupee take you farther. Let every journey begin with Travelxploria.
